questions.question: As per Solvent Extractors’ Association, the import of which product has decreased by 12 per cent during this oil year in India?
questions.explanation: As per the recent statement of the Solvent Extractors’ Association (SEA) of India, the import of edible oils to the country reduced by 12 per cent in the first 11 months (November to September) of the oil year 2019-20.The oil year ranges from November to October. India imported 11.95 million tonnes of edible oil during November-September of 2019-20 as against 13.58 million tonnes during the corresponding period of 2018-19.

questions.question: What is ‘OSIRIS-Rex’ that was seen in news recently?
questions.explanation: Origins, Spectral Interpretation, Resource Identification, Security, Regolith Explorer (OSIRIS-REx) is a space craft of the NASA.It was seen in news recently as the space craft touched an asteroid using its robotic arm to collect dust and pebbles from the surface to deliver to Earth in 2023. The asteroid known as Bennu, is currently more than 321 million kilometers from Earth.

questions.question: Which state has the best Anaemia Mukt Bharat (AMB) Index among 29 states in the country?
questions.explanation: In a recently released scorecard of the Anaemia Mukt Bharat (AMB) Index by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Haryana was ranked at the top spot with an AMB Index of 46.7.The Anaemia Mukt Bharat is an initiative of the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare and UNICEF. It aims to reduce the prevalence of Anaemia all over India.

questions.question: The Union Cabinet has approved MoU with which country on cooperation in space exploration?
questions.explanation: The Union Cabinet has approved the Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) between India and Nigeria on cooperation in the exploration and using outer space for peaceful purposes.The MoU has been signed between Indian Space Research Organisation and the National Space Research and Development Agency (NASRDA) of Nigeria. The countries will set up a joint working group to implement the process.

questions.question: Which organisation has set up a committee under Umesh Sinha and Harish Kumar?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India has recently set up a committee under its Director General Umesh Sinha and ex Indian Revenue Service officer Harish Kumar.The panel has been set up to examine the existing expenditure limits for election candidates amidst the increase in number of electors and rise in Cost Inflation Index. Earlier, a 10% increase in the expenditure limit of election candidates.

questions.question: Which country has given complete approval for using Antiviral Drug Remdesivir to treat COVID-19?
questions.explanation: The US Food and Drug Administration has recently granted full approval to the antiviral drug Remdesivir as a treatment for patients hospitalized with Covid-19.The organisation had given conditional authorization in May. USFDA also announced that the drug, sold under the brand name Veklury, was the only specific treatment for Covid-19 approved so far.

questions.question: What is the name of the app launched by Vodafone Idea and Nasscom Foundation for women safety?
questions.explanation: Vodafone Idea and Nasscom Foundation has launched an app named “My Ambar” (meaning my sky). It is an app-based solution for women safety in India. It has been developed under the ‘Connecting for Good program’. The app aims to help women understand and raise their voice against violence. The app is available in English and Hindi. It is provided with easy access to key helpline numbers and service providers across the country.

questions.question: Which country has recently passed a controversial 20th constitution amendment?
questions.explanation: Sri Lankan parliament has recently passed the controversial 20th Amendment to its constitution. It was passed with two-thirds majority, after a two-day long debate. The amendment provides for greater immunity for the Executive President and provides with immense powers to the President. Eight opposition MPs voted in favour of the legislation, which was opposed by their party and challenged in Sri Lanka’s Supreme Court.

questions.question: Which state became the third State to curtail the Central Bureau of Investigation’s (CBI) powers?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra became the third state in the country to curtail the Central Bureau of Investigation’s (CBI) powers in the state. The state withdrew its general consent agreement with the CBI agency. Hence forth CBI has to seek permission from the Government of Maharashtra for every case it has to investigate. A similar move was earlier done by Rajasthan and West Bengal.

questions.question: Which Public Sector Company has been granted permission to use drones for inspection activities?
questions.explanation: NTPC Limited has been permitted to use Remotely Piloted Aircraft System (RPAS) or drones, for carrying out research and inspection activities. The permission has been granted by the Ministry of Civil Aviation (MoCA) and Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA). NTPC would use drones at Vindhyachal Super Thermal Power Station, Madhya Pradesh, Gadarwara Super Thermal Power Plant, Madhya Pradesh, and Sipat Super Thermal Power Project, Chhattisgarh.
